digest.greeting: Friday, June 12, Jean. Outlook shows 8 working-day calendar blocks, with the main operating run from the 9:30 AM TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint through the 3:00 PM Executive Project Status Slide Update; Phase 2, CoE, EPMO EOW, and the executive deck are today’s strongest anchors. There is 1 carried-over item, 1 reminder, and 5 high-priority live actions: close the HR Mid-Year Touchpoints before Monday’s deadline, keep Phase 2 SMS approvals and UAT testers moving, approve Camilo’s corrected leave request, use the EPMO portal reminder, and capture BCP/IR follow-through. Teams recent-thread discovery still returned a Microsoft Graph 403, so Teams context is limited to targeted search and Teams-notification signals.

items.label: Close BCP/IR and portfolio 1:1 follow-through
items.detail: Carried over from Thursday and still not marked complete in GitHub state. Convert any BCP/Incident Response, Camilo, or Maria 1:1 outcomes into named owners, blockers, or next steps before today’s EPMO and executive-status meetings.

items.label: Revisar EPMO portal
items.detail: One-shot reminder from dashboard state. Use the 12:00 PM blocked space or 2:30 PM focus window to review the EPMO portal before the EPMO EOW check-in and executive deck update.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. Friday is front-loaded with project syncs, then splits into EPMO review and executive deck work after lunch.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Short Yuri touchpoint. Good place to close TRACE or Staff Aug deltas before the Phase 2 and CoE blocks.

items.label: Phase 2 Weekly IT Projects Sync
items.detail: Primary morning anchor. Jhara declined while Yuri, Andrea, Mar, David, Santiago, Anu, and others are listed; bring the Phase 2 SMS approval, UAT-tester, and Sprint 14 dependencies.

items.label: CoE Sync Jean
items.detail: Mar-organized CoE sync with Jean and David. Use for CoE routing, Phase 2 SMS next steps, and any intake or ownership decisions that need alignment.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Protected hour after the morning syncs. Best use is Mid-Year Touchpoints, EPMO portal review, and Phase 2 SMS cleanup while the context is fresh.

items.label: EPMO EOW Check In
items.detail: Sara moved this one hour earlier and added Nadia for programmatic updates. Bring Phase 2/Nadia context and any EPMO portal observations that need leadership visibility.

items.label: Focus time
items.detail: Thirty-minute cleanup window between EPMO EOW and Santiago. Use only for the highest-friction item: Rippling reviews, leave approval, or executive deck prep.

items.label: 1:1 Santiago & Jean
items.detail: Compressed handoff before the deck update. Useful for Staff Aug, Incident Response, or portfolio follow-through that needs Santiago’s input.

items.label: Executive Project Status Slide Update
items.detail: Weekly executive-project deck update with Yuri, Jhara, and Mar. The invite links the Executive Project Status Weekly Update deck; use the session to make leadership-facing status crisp before the weekend.

items.label: Focus time
items.detail: Ninety-minute end-of-day block after the deck update. Best for sending follow-ups, closing Rippling items, and logging next-week owners.

items.label: Submit Mid-Year Touchpoints by Monday
items.detail: Action type: review/submit. HR’s unread June 11 email asks Jean to submit Mid-Year Touchpoints in Rippling for Camilo Vejarano, Andrea Rodriguez, David Molano, and Mar Garcia by Monday, June 15, including KPIs and competencies; HR notes completion rates are still low across the organization.

items.label: Advance Phase 2 SMS approvals and UAT owners
items.detail: Action type: follow up/decide. Carolina’s latest unread reply says scope was updated, SMS scripts were sent to Andrew in Legal and Marketing, Marketing is due June 16, and Jessica/Craig are assigned as testers for now. Use the Phase 2 and CoE blocks to confirm remaining Legal-first approval, UAT owner, AOS owner, and scope-update gaps before the June 24 Sprint 14 cutoff.

items.label: Approve Camilo’s corrected leave request
items.detail: Action type: approve. Camilo messaged that his original vacation request accidentally covered a family day; Rippling now has an approval request for 7 paid vacation days from June 17 to June 25, leaving 2.25 days after the request. Decide whether to approve the corrected request today so coverage can be planned before Monday.

items.label: Prepare EPMO EOW and executive deck updates
items.detail: Action type: prepare. Today’s EPMO EOW Check In adds Nadia for programmatic updates and flows into the Executive Project Status Slide Update. Bring the Phase 2 SMS state, Nadia/Steering Committee implications, and any BCP/IR carry-over that needs executive-facing status.

items.label: Plan Lead Up Module 2 pre-work
items.detail: Action type: schedule/review. Rippling enrolled Jean in Lead up Module 2 Pre Work with a due date of June 25 at 8:06 AM. This is not a same-day emergency, but put it on the radar before next week fills up.

items.label: HR is waiting on Mid-Year Touchpoints
items.detail: Unread from HR on June 11, copied to Sara. It specifically names Camilo Vejarano, Andrea Rodriguez, David Molano, and Mar Garcia, and asks for KPIs plus competencies in Rippling by Monday, June 15.

items.label: Carolina replied on Phase 2 SMS execution
items.detail: Unread reply at 6:28 PM on June 11. The thread now has updated scope, Legal and Marketing approval motion, a June 16 Marketing due date, and provisional UAT testers; it is directly relevant to today’s Phase 2 and CoE meetings.

items.label: Rippling has Camilo’s corrected leave approval
items.detail: Unread Rippling approval request for Camilo’s June 17-25 vacation. This supersedes the earlier June 16 approval signal because Camilo clarified the family-day conflict in Teams and resubmitted.

items.label: Camilo clarified the leave-date correction
items.detail: Targeted Teams search found Camilo’s message from June 11 explaining that June 16 was already approved as family day, so the vacation should run June 17-25. This supports approving the corrected Rippling request rather than acting on the old date range.

items.label: Check the Weekly Executive Project Updates thread
items.detail: Action type: review. Targeted Teams search surfaced recent activity in the Weekly Executive Project Updates chat tied to today’s deck work, including a technical-access troubleshooting exchange. Scan it before the 3:00 PM deck update in case it affects the executive-status workflow.

items.label: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week
items.detail: Monday starts with a one-hour prep block. Use it to confirm any remaining Mid-Year Touchpoint submissions due that same day.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Short Yuri touchpoint. Good place to close Staff Aug or TRACE follow-ups left from Friday.

items.label: 1:1 Shelley & Jean
items.detail: Agenda says the purpose is to understand department needs and how Jean can support. Relevant if Phase 2 SMS, USCIS Interviews, or Bonafide EAD scope needs Shelley input.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Protected hour before EPMO Portfolio Review. Best used for any remaining Rippling or Phase 2 cleanup.

items.label: EPMO Portfolio Review
items.detail: Sara’s portfolio review focuses on new project requests, EPMO classification, missing info, ownership, and rejection communication. Bring any Phase 2 or CoE intake items that need portfolio-level routing.

reminderHistory.text: Seguimiento a stakeholders de Co-counsel network project. Go/no-go?
